canonical_question: What are Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) capabilities - message packs, adapters, Gen 3 migration?
aliases: Oracle OIC Gen 3 features and limitations
OIC message pack pricing and billing model
Oracle Integration Cloud adapter catalog and integration patterns
OIC Gen 2 vs Gen 3 upgrade differences

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Gen 3 migration (2024) - OAuth replaces basic auth, connectivity agent changes

constraints: Message packs: minimum 1 pack (5K msgs/hr standard, 20K msgs/hr BYOL); billed per hour whether messages are sent or not
Standard edition max 12 packs (60K msgs/hr); BYOL max 3 packs (60K msgs/hr)
Concurrent sync requests: 100 per message pack, max 2,000 total
File Server concurrent connections: 50 per service instance
Gen 3 Projects: max 100 integrations, 50 deployments, 20 connections, 20 lookups, 20 JS libraries per project
Enterprise edition required for on-premise adapters (E-Business Suite, JD Edwards, Siebel, SAP) and Process Automation
Gen 3 Projects cannot import standalone integration assets or use cross-project invocation

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs Oracle ERP Cloud REST API endpoints and rate limits
User needs Oracle ERP Cloud FBDI file import details
User needs Oracle ERP Cloud authentication flows
User needs SAP Integration Suite comparison

related_to.label: Oracle ERP Cloud REST API Capabilities

often_confused_with.label: Oracle ERP Cloud REST API (the ERP endpoints, not the iPaaS platform)

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
Which OIC edition are you using?
What system are you integrating with?
options: Oracle SaaS (ERP Cloud, HCM, SCM, CX)
On-premise (E-Business Suite, JD Edwards, SAP ECC)
